source_test: HEC.FDA.Model/metrics/AggregatedConsequencesByQuantile.cs (no upstream unit test file exists for this class; oracle-gate-reproduced against the real AggregatedConsequencesByQuantile/Empirical directly)
note: construct = {damage_category, asset_category, empirical: {probabilities, values}, impact_area_id, consequence_type, risk_type}, matching the AggregatedConsequencesByQuantile(string, string, Empirical, int, ConsequenceType, RiskType) ctor; the Empirical is built via the two-array (probabilities, values) ctor. `consequence_type`/`risk_type` are the C# enum member names (ConsequenceType: UNASSIGNED/Damage/LifeLoss/All; RiskType: Fail/Non_Fail/Total/Unassigned). Assertion `method` dispatches consequence_sample_mean (args []) -> Empirical.SampleMean (0.0, never set by either ported ctor -- see empirical.hpp; kept as a case to pin the faithful zero rather than assuming it), or consequence_exceeded_with_probability_q (args [q]) -> Empirical.InverseCDF(1-q). Cases: (1) uniform_deciles -- a 10-point evenly-spaced Empirical (probabilities 0.1..1.0, values 100..1000), Damage/Fail; non-exceedance probability 1-0.5=0.5 lands exactly on an interior CumulativeProbabilities grid point (0.5), exercising InverseCDF's exact-hit-via-Array.BinarySearch branch; (2) narrow_three_point -- a 3-point Empirical (probabilities 0.2/0.6/0.9, values 10/25/60), LifeLoss/Total; non-exceedance 0.5 falls strictly between the 0.2 and 0.6 grid points, exercising InverseCDF's interpolation branch instead.
